Effect kinds: `persistence`, `notification`, `invocation`, `tool`. I'll use `invocation`.

```dcl
language dcl 1.0

actor ApiClient is system

shape UptimeRequest {
  // GET /uptime has no body, but DCL requires a shape for intent
  placeholder: Boolean
}

event UptimeReported is {
  service: Text required
  started_at: DateTime required
  uptime_seconds: Number required
}

effect LogUptimeQuery is invocation

policy UptimePerformance {
  performance {
    budget 100ms
  }
}

capability GetUptime {
  intent UptimeRequest from ApiClient

  outcomes {
    UptimeReturned
  }

  effects {
    LogUptimeQuery
  }

  events {
    emits UptimeReported
  }

  policies {
    UptimePerformance governs capability
    UptimePerformance governs lifecycle
  }

  observe {
    capability duration as uptime_query_duration
    effect LogUptimeQuery count as uptime_query_count
  }

  when {
    otherwise then UptimeReturned
  }

  lifecycle {
    begin Pending
    step Processing
    end Completed
    move Pending to Completed on outcome UptimeReturned
  }
}
```
